2603.13595.title: A Causal Framework for Mitigating Data Shifts in Healthcare
2603.13595.paper_summary: Developing predictive models that perform reliably across diverse patient populations and heterogeneous environments is a core aim of medical research. However, generalization is only possible if the learned model is robust to statistical differences between data used for training and data seen at the time and place of deployment. Domain generalization methods provide strategies to address data shifts, but each method comes with its own set of assumptions and trade-offs. To apply these methods in healthcare, we must understand how domain shifts arise, what assumptions we prefer to make, and what our design constraints are. This article proposes a causal framework for the design of predictive models to improve generalization. Causality provides a powerful language to characterize and understand diverse domain shifts, regardless of data modality. This allows us to pinpoint why models fail to generalize, leading to more principled strategies to prepare for and adapt to shifts. We recommend general mitigation strategies, discussing trade-offs and highlighting existing work. Our causality-based perspective offers a critical foundation for developing robust, interpretable, and clinically relevant AI solutions in healthcare, paving the way for reliable real-world deployment.
2603.13595.paper_summary_zh: 開發能在多樣化病人群體和異質環境中可靠運作的預測模型是醫學研究的核心目標。<lb/>然而，只有當學習到的模型對於訓練數據和部署時所見數據之間的統計差異具有穩健性時，泛化才是可能的。<lb/>領域泛化方法提供了解決數據轉變的策略，但每種方法都有其自身的一組假設和權衡。<lb/>要在醫療保健中應用這些方法，我們必須了解領域轉變是如何產生的，我們希望做出什麼假設，以及我們的設計限制是什麼。<lb/>本文提出了一個因果框架，用於設計預測模型以改善泛化能力。<lb/>因果關係提供了一種強大的語言，用於描述和理解多樣的領域轉變，無論數據模式如何。<lb/>這使我們能夠確定模型為何無法泛化，從而導致更有原則的策略來準備和適應轉變。<lb/>我們建議一般的緩解策略，討論權衡並突顯現有的工作。<lb/>我們基於因果關係的觀點為開發穩健、可解釋且與臨床相關的人工智慧解決方案在醫療保健領域提供了重要的基礎，為可靠的現實世界部署鋪平了道路。
2603.13595.author: Kurt Butler et.al.
2603.13595.authors: Kurt Butler, Stephanie Riley, Damian Machlanski, Edward Moroshko, Panagiotis Dimitrakopoulos, Thomas Melistas, Akchunya Chanchal, Konstantinos Vilouras, Zhihua Liu, Steven McDonagh, Hana Chockler, Ben Glocker, Niccolo Tempini, Matthew Sperrin, Sotirios A Tsaftaris, Ricardo Silva
